title: Quick Dominican Citrus-Garlic Pollo with Brown Rice & Black Beans (Serves 2)
summary: Lean, Dominican-inspired weeknight dinner: citrus-garlic pan-braised chicken in a light achiote-sofrito sauce, served with a controlled 1/2 cup brown rice and 1/3 cup black beans per person plus a crisp tomato-cabbage sofrito salad. Low-sodium and ready in ~30 minutes; estimated carbs ≈ 34–39 g/serving (verify with a scale or app).

recipe.title: Quick Dominican Citrus-Garlic Pollo with Brown Rice & Black Beans (Serves 2)
recipe.summary: Lean, Dominican-inspired weeknight dinner: citrus-garlic pan-braised chicken in a light achiote-sofrito sauce, served with a controlled 1/2 cup brown rice and 1/3 cup black beans per person plus a crisp tomato-cabbage sofrito salad. Low-sodium and ready in ~30 minutes; estimated carbs ≈ 34–39 g/serving (verify with a scale or app).
ingredients: 2 boneless skinless chicken breasts (about 10–12 oz total; ~5–6 oz each)
1 tsp orange juice (or 1/2 tsp orange zest)
1/2 tsp smoked paprika or 1/4 tsp achiote (annatto) powder
1 small red bell pepper, thinly sliced
1 cup low-sodium chicken broth or water (prefer low-sodium)
1 cup cooked brown rice (measured cooked = total; serve 1/2 cup per person)
2/3 cup low-sodium canned black beans, rinsed and drained (~1/3 cup per serving)
1 cup shredded cabbage or thinly sliced green cabbage
2 tbsp chopped cilantro (or culantro if available)
1 tsp white or apple cider vinegar
Optional: sliced avocado (1/4 medium per serving) — counts toward carbs/fat
steps: Prep: Rinse and pat chicken dry. Slice breasts in half horizontally if thick to make 2 thin cutlets for faster cooking.
Marinade (5 minutes): In a bowl combine garlic, lime juice, orange, black pepper, and achiote/paprika. Toss chicken to coat and let rest while heating pan.
Cook rice/beans: If rice is not already cooked, use quick-cook brown rice or microwave pre-cooked rice. Warm rinsed low-sodium black beans in a small pot with 2 tbsp water; keep covered and low heat.
Sear chicken (6–8 minutes): Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Add chicken and sear 2–3 minutes per side until golden. Remove and set aside.

Make quick sofrito sauce (4–5 minutes): In same skillet add sliced onion and bell pepper; sauté 3 minutes until softened. Add diced tomato and 1 cup low-sodium broth (or water), scrape browned bits, simmer 2 minutes and return chicken to pan. Cover and simmer 4–6 minutes until chicken reaches 165°F (74°C).
Salad (while chicken cooks, 3 minutes): Toss shredded cabbage, chopped cilantro, 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp vinegar, and a pinch of black pepper; finish with a squeeze of lime.

Plate: Divide 1/2 cup cooked brown rice between two plates, spoon 1/3 cup warmed black beans next to rice, place one chicken piece with some sofrito sauce on top, and add a generous serving of the cabbage-tomato salad. Serve with lime wedge and optional 1/4 avocado slice per person.
Taste and adjust: For low-sodium preference, do not add table salt; if needed, add a tiny pinch of salt per person after tasting.
substitutions: Chicken: swap with 6 oz firm white fish (tilapia/dorado) — pan-sear 2–3 minutes per side; or 6 oz firm tofu (press and sear) for plant-based protein.
Rice: substitute 1 cup cooked cauliflower rice (lower carbs) or 1/2 cup cooked white rice if preferred (adjust carb estimate).
Beans: use low-sodium red kidney or pigeon peas (habichuelas rojas/gandules) rinsed and drained.
Achiote: if unavailable, use 1/2 tsp smoked paprika + tiny pinch turmeric for color and flavor.
Cilantro: substitute parsley if culantro/cilantro unavailable.
No-oil option: use a nonstick skillet and reduce oil to 1 tsp; finish salad with a splash of lime instead of oil.

warnings: Nutrition estimates are approximate — verify carbohydrate amounts with a food scale or trusted nutrition app before using for carbohydrate counting or insulin dosing.

This guidance is not medical advice. Do not change insulin, medication, or clinical care based on this recipe; consult a clinician or registered dietitian for individualized dosing or medical decisions.

People using insulin or other glucose-lowering medications should monitor blood glucose closely after trying a new meal and follow clinician guidance for dosing — do not assume the estimate replaces professional advice.
Confirm product sodium on labels; 'low-sodium' canned goods and broths vary — rinse canned beans to reduce sodium further.
Ensure poultry reaches a safe internal temperature (165°F / 74°C) to prevent foodborne illness.
Watch for individual food allergies or intolerances (e.g., avocado, legumes) and substitute as needed.
Estimate carbs and nutrition; recommend using a food scale or nutrition app to confirm amounts for people counting carbs.
Avoid recommending insulin or medication changes; encourage consultation with a clinician or registered dietitian for individualized dosing and medical guidance.
For low-sodium preference, use no-salt seasoning blends, low-sodium canned beans (or rinse/wash beans), and omit added salt; taste before adding salt.
safety_review.disclaimer: This recipe is for general wellness and meal-planning support only. It is not medical advice and does not replace guidance from a clinician or registered dietitian.

title: Quick Dominican-Style Light Pollo Guisado with Cauli-Quinoa Rice
summary: A 20–30 minute Dominican-inspired one-pan dinner: lean chicken simmered in a low-sodium sofrito-tomato sauce with peppers and zucchini, served with a controlled portion of cauliflower+quinoa rice and a small side of black beans—balanced for protein, fiber, and lower carbs (~30 g carbs/serving, within the 45 g limit).

recipe.title: Quick Dominican-Style Light Pollo Guisado with Cauli-Quinoa Rice
recipe.summary: A 20–30 minute Dominican-inspired one-pan dinner: lean chicken simmered in a low-sodium sofrito-tomato sauce with peppers and zucchini, served with a controlled portion of cauliflower+quinoa rice and a small side of black beans—balanced for protein, fiber, and lower carbs (~30 g carbs/serving, within the 45 g limit).
ingredients: 300 g skinless boneless chicken breast or thigh, cut into 4–6 pieces (≈150 g per serving)
2 tbsp low-sodium sofrito (recaito) or homemade mix of cilantro, onion, garlic, and green pepper
1/4 cup low-sodium chicken broth or water
1 tsp annatto (achiote) or 1/4 tsp smoked paprika for color
1/2 cup cooked quinoa (measured cooked) or 1/4 cup dry quinoa, cooked
2 cups riced cauliflower (fresh or frozen, thawed and squeezed dry)
Fresh cilantro or culantro, chopped, for garnish
Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
steps: Prep: pat chicken dry, season lightly with black pepper, 1/2 tsp cumin and 1/4 tsp oregano; cook quinoa per package if not already cooked.
Heat oil in a wide skillet over medium-high. Sear chicken 2 minutes per side until lightly browned; remove and set aside.
Lower heat to medium. Add onion and bell pepper; sauté 3 minutes until softened. Stir in sofrito, annatto/paprika, remaining cumin and oregano; cook 30–45 seconds to bloom aromas.

Add crushed tomato and broth, scrape browned bits, return chicken to the pan. Cover and simmer 8–10 minutes until chicken is cooked through and sauce thickens. Finish with lime zest and 1 tbsp lime juice. Taste and adjust with more lime or pepper (avoid adding salt).

While chicken simmers, make cauli-quinoa rice: in a separate skillet over medium, add 1 tsp oil, sauté riced cauliflower 3–4 minutes. Stir in cooked quinoa and 1–2 tbsp chopped cilantro, cook 1–2 minutes until warmed; season with pepper and a squeeze of lime.
Warm the black beans in a small pot with a splash of water and a pinch of cumin; drain excess liquid.

Plate: divide cauli-quinoa rice between two plates (~3/4 cup total per person), top with stewed chicken and vegetables, and serve a 1/4 cup portion of black beans on the side. Garnish with chopped cilantro and extra lime wedges.
substitutions: Chicken: substitute firm white fish (e.g., cod) — reduce simmer time to 4–6 minutes.
Quinoa: replace with 1/2 cup cooked brown rice if preferred (increase carbs — adjust portions to stay under limit).
Cauliflower rice: use extra cooked cauliflower or 1 cup steamed broccoli rice for similar fiber and low carbs.
Black beans: swap for 1/4 cup cooked pigeon peas (gandules) for a more traditional flavor; watch portion size for carbs.
Sofrito: if unavailable, use 1 tbsp finely chopped cilantro + 1 tbsp finely chopped green bell pepper + 1 small minced garlic clove.

warnings: Nutrition estimates are approximate; individual blood glucose responses vary—monitor glucose and adjust portions as needed.
Do not change medications or insulin based on this recipe; consult a clinician or registered dietitian for dosing guidance.
Sodium may be higher if using regular canned tomatoes, broths, or condiments—use low-sodium products and check labels.
If you have kidney disease, heart disease, or other medical conditions, confirm sodium and protein targets with a healthcare professional.
Beans, quinoa, and rice portions significantly affect total carbs—measure portions carefully to stay within the per-serving carb limit.
safety_review.disclaimer: This recipe is for general wellness and meal-planning support only. It is not medical advice and does not replace guidance from a clinician or registered dietitian.

steps: Prep: pat chicken dry, season lightly with black pepper, 1/2 tsp cumin and 1/4 tsp oregano; cook quinoa per package if not already cooked.
Heat oil in a wide skillet over medium-high. Sear chicken 2 minutes per side until lightly browned; remove and set aside.
Lower heat to medium. Add onion and bell pepper; sauté 3 minutes until softened. Stir in sofrito, annatto/paprika, remaining cumin and oregano; cook 30–45 seconds to bloom aromas.

Add crushed tomato and broth, scrape browned bits, return chicken to the pan. Cover and simmer 8–10 minutes until chicken is cooked through and sauce thickens. Finish with lime zest and 1 tbsp lime juice. Taste and adjust with more lime or pepper (avoid adding salt).

While chicken simmers, make cauli-quinoa rice: in a separate skillet over medium, add 1 tsp oil, sauté riced cauliflower 3–4 minutes. Stir in cooked quinoa and 1–2 tbsp chopped cilantro, cook 1–2 minutes until warmed; season with pepper and a squeeze of lime.
Warm the black beans in a small pot with a splash of water and a pinch of cumin; drain excess liquid.

Plate: divide cauli-quinoa rice between two plates (~3/4 cup total per person), top with stewed chicken and vegetables, and serve a 1/4 cup portion of black beans on the side. Garnish with chopped cilantro and extra lime wedges.
substitutions: Chicken: substitute firm white fish (e.g., cod) — reduce simmer time to 4–6 minutes.
Quinoa: replace with 1/2 cup cooked brown rice if preferred (increase carbs — adjust portions to stay under limit).
Cauliflower rice: use extra cooked cauliflower or 1 cup steamed broccoli rice for similar fiber and low carbs.
Black beans: swap for 1/4 cup cooked pigeon peas (gandules) for a more traditional flavor; watch portion size for carbs.
Sofrito: if unavailable, use 1 tbsp finely chopped cilantro + 1 tbsp finely chopped green bell pepper + 1 small minced garlic clove.

warnings: Nutrition estimates are approximate; individual blood glucose responses vary—monitor glucose and adjust portions as needed.
Do not change medications or insulin based on this recipe; consult a clinician or registered dietitian for dosing guidance.

safety_review.disclaimer: This recipe is for general wellness and meal-planning support only. It is not medical advice and does not replace guidance from a clinician or registered dietitian.

title: Quick Dominican-Style Light Pollo Guisado with Cauli-Quinoa Rice
summary: A 20–30 minute Dominican-inspired one-pan dinner: lean chicken simmered in a low-sodium sofrito-tomato sauce with peppers and zucchini, served with a controlled portion of cauliflower+quinoa rice and a small side of black beans—balanced for protein, fiber, and lower carbs (~30 g carbs/serving, within the 45 g limit).

recipe.title: Quick Dominican-Style Light Pollo Guisado with Cauli-Quinoa Rice
recipe.summary: A 20–30 minute Dominican-inspired one-pan dinner: lean chicken simmered in a low-sodium sofrito-tomato sauce with peppers and zucchini, served with a controlled portion of cauliflower+quinoa rice and a small side of black beans—balanced for protein, fiber, and lower carbs (~30 g carbs/serving, within the 45 g limit).
ingredients: 300 g skinless boneless chicken breast or thigh, cut into 4–6 pieces (≈150 g per serving)
2 tbsp low-sodium sofrito (recaito) or homemade mix of cilantro, onion, garlic, and green pepper
1/4 cup low-sodium chicken broth or water
1 tsp annatto (achiote) or 1/4 tsp smoked paprika for color
1/2 cup cooked quinoa (measured cooked) or 1/4 cup dry quinoa, cooked
2 cups riced cauliflower (fresh or frozen, thawed and squeezed dry)
Fresh cilantro or culantro, chopped, for garnish
Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
steps: Prep: pat chicken dry, season lightly with black pepper, 1/2 tsp cumin and 1/4 tsp oregano; cook quinoa per package if not already cooked.
Heat oil in a wide skillet over medium-high. Sear chicken 2 minutes per side until lightly browned; remove and set aside.
Lower heat to medium. Add onion and bell pepper; sauté 3 minutes until softened. Stir in sofrito, annatto/paprika, remaining cumin and oregano; cook 30–45 seconds to bloom aromas.

Add crushed tomato and broth, scrape browned bits, return chicken to the pan. Cover and simmer 8–10 minutes until chicken is cooked through and sauce thickens. Finish with lime zest and 1 tbsp lime juice. Taste and adjust with more lime or pepper (avoid adding salt).

While chicken simmers, make cauli-quinoa rice: in a separate skillet over medium, add 1 tsp oil, sauté riced cauliflower 3–4 minutes. Stir in cooked quinoa and 1–2 tbsp chopped cilantro, cook 1–2 minutes until warmed; season with pepper and a squeeze of lime.
Warm the black beans in a small pot with a splash of water and a pinch of cumin; drain excess liquid.

Plate: divide cauli-quinoa rice between two plates (~3/4 cup total per person), top with stewed chicken and vegetables, and serve a 1/4 cup portion of black beans on the side. Garnish with chopped cilantro and extra lime wedges.
substitutions: Chicken: substitute firm white fish (e.g., cod) — reduce simmer time to 4–6 minutes.
Quinoa: replace with 1/2 cup cooked brown rice if preferred (increase carbs — adjust portions to stay under limit).
Cauliflower rice: use extra cooked cauliflower or 1 cup steamed broccoli rice for similar fiber and low carbs.
Black beans: swap for 1/4 cup cooked pigeon peas (gandules) for a more traditional flavor; watch portion size for carbs.
Sofrito: if unavailable, use 1 tbsp finely chopped cilantro + 1 tbsp finely chopped green bell pepper + 1 small minced garlic clove.

warnings: Nutrition estimates are approximate; individual blood glucose responses vary—monitor glucose and adjust portions as needed.
Do not change medications or insulin based on this recipe; consult a clinician or registered dietitian for dosing guidance.
Sodium may be higher if using regular canned tomatoes, broths, or condiments—use low-sodium products and check labels.
If you have kidney disease, heart disease, or other medical conditions, confirm sodium and protein targets with a healthcare professional.
Beans, quinoa, and rice portions significantly affect total carbs—measure portions carefully to stay within the per-serving carb limit.
safety_review.disclaimer: This recipe is for general wellness and meal-planning support only. It is not medical advice and does not replace guidance from a clinician or registered dietitian.
